narrative: The selloff that began Tuesday accelerated overnight: bitcoin briefly fell below $62,000 — its lowest since February — as $1.5 billion in long positions were liquidated, per CoinDesk. Charles Schwab's Ferraioli attributes the weakness not to Saylor selling but to momentum rotation into AI stocks and blockbuster IPOs, while BTC/ETH/SOL/XRP ETFs bled $4.4 billion across 13 straight sessions. Against the price drop, stablecoin infrastructure kept building: Stripe, Visa, and Mastercard are said to be backing a new stablecoin platform (per CoinDesk), and Franklin Templeton launched 24/7 institutional yield swaps via MoonPay.
worth_watching: SpaceX IPO (targeting $75B) — the momentum rotation narrative points here; a blockbuster debut could sustain crypto outflows as capital chases the new trade
Hyperliquid (HYPE) — the only major crypto ETF category still attracting net new money as BTC/ETH/SOL/XRP funds bleed; institutional attention growing
Bitmine (BMNR) — Tom Lee's Ethereum treasury firm nearing $9B paper loss as ETH stays below $1,800; watch for forced selling or restructuring risk
